total_stack_advantage.interpretation:
Grover's optimal unstructured search needs 51,471 iterations on 2^32 states.
After the full HENDRIX-Φ + PULVINI stack reduces effective dimension from 32 to 21.7 bits (10.3-bit reduction, ~1.3e+03× space reduction),
structured Grover needs only 1,448 iterations — a 35.5× advantage over plain Grover.

Conservative minimum (manifold + expander + baseline grad only): ~4.8e+01× (1.7 orders of magnitude).

Each layer is independently proven by:
  - Empirical blockchain measurement (96 blocks, z=8.16)
  - Algebraic proof (phi-folding invertibility)
  - Established theorem (quantum walk on expander)
  - Empirical benchmark (+2.84% Φ/step vs linear)
  - Deterministic construction (phi scaling ensemble)
